records.ety: [L. excrementum, fr. excernere, excretum, to skin out, discharge: cf. F. excrment. See Excrete.]

defs: Matter excreted and ejected; that which is excreted or cast out of the animal body by any of the natural emunctories; especially, alvine, discharges; dung; ordure.

records.ety: [L. excrementum, fr. excrescere, excretum, to grow out. See Excrescence.]

defs: An excrescence or appendage; an outgrowth.
quotes: Living creatures put forth (after their period of growth) nothing that is young but hair and nails, which are excrements and no parts.
